title: Hello, C#
instruction:
Every C# program starts at the `Main` method inside a class.

`Console.WriteLine()` prints a line. Run this.
successMessage: You just ran a C# program. The using System line imports the standard library. class Program is the container. static void Main() is where execution begins.

title: Print multiple lines
instruction:
Each `Console.WriteLine()` prints a separate line.

Add two more lines and run it.
hint: Add two more Console.WriteLine("text"); lines inside Main.

codeChecks.message: Add two more Console.WriteLine calls — three total.

successMessage: Three lines, three outputs. C# executes them top to bottom.

title: Print with Write (no newline)
instruction:
`Console.Write()` prints without adding a newline. Use it to build output piece by piece.

Run this.
successMessage: Write does not add a newline. The final WriteLine does. Together they build a single line from parts.

title: Print numbers and variables
instruction:
C# can print numbers directly. Use `{}` as a format placeholder:

```csharp
Console.WriteLine("Value: {0}", 42);
```

Run this.
successMessage: C# booleans print as True/False (capitalised). {0} in a format string is replaced by the first argument after the format.

title: String interpolation
successMessage: String interpolation with $ is the modern C# way. You can put any expression inside {}, not just variable names.

title: Comments
instruction:
C# has three comment styles:
- `// single line`
- `/* multi-line */`
- `/// XML doc comment`

Run this.
successMessage: Comments are stripped by the compiler. Write them to explain WHY, not what.

title: Fix the syntax error
instruction: This program has a bug. Run it, read the error, and fix it.
hint: Check the string — count the quote characters carefully.
successMessage: C# compiler errors are detailed. Learning to read them quickly is a core skill.

title: Write from scratch
instruction:
Write a C# program that prints:
- `C# is modern`
- `C# is fast`
- `C# is fun`
hint: Add three Console.WriteLine calls inside Main.
successMessage: You wrote a complete C# program from scratch. The template — using System, class, Main — will become second nature.
